title: Validate Python generated CLI boundary issue #954

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Ingest and validate GitHub #954: the Python generated CLI completion boundary must be internally consistent and must not overclaim generated ownership.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Treat GitHub #954 as intake, not execution authority. First verify the reported root-runtime and parser-ownership claims against the current branch before changing product code.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Whether the issue resolves as stale evidence, contract wording, static proof, runtime entrypoint repair, or a narrower follow-up inside the Python codegen lane.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.
canonical_core.next_action: Verify #954's reported evidence against the current tree and decide the smallest product correction.
proof_expectations: uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json
touched_scope: packages/*/src/*/_runtime_cli.py only if static proof or runtime-boundary wording requires it
completion_criteria: #954 is reconciled against current code; any real runtime failure or overclaim is fixed or routed to a narrower checked-in continuation; the issue is updated with the verified finding and proof.

goal: Validate whether #954 exposes a real Python generated CLI completion defect, then fix or route the verified finding.
non_goals: Do not restart the full Python codegen epic.
Do not touch TypeScript, Bash, or PowerShell target conformance.
Do not implement broad parser extraction unless the static proof shows the completion gate is actually overclaiming.
